The Boolean hypercube of size n by mapping f(xi) to 1 and g(xi) to 0 for all i, indicating the size of the largest Boolean cube contained in H, which is the Natarajan dimension. Essentially, every coordinate i gets its own translation of the 0, 1 labels of the Boolean cube.

The set S contains the set {f(xi), g(xi)} × ... × {f(xn), g(xn)} which is isomorphic to the Boolean hypercube of size n by mapping f(xi) to 1 and g(xi) to 0 for all i. This means that the Natarajan dimension is essentially the size of the largest Boolean cube contained in H.

The gamma-Natarajan dimension is defined as the scaled analogue of the Natarajan dimension. It indicates the size of the largest Boolean cube that is contained in H. Essentially, every coordinate i gets its own translation of the 0, 1 labels of the Boolean cube, with the requirement that these two labels are at least 2gamma far from each other.
